steps.action: Open Budget panel and create Engineering envelope with $1,000,000 and headcount cap 5
steps.expected: Envelope is created with configured values
steps.observed: Engineering envelope created; org-wide overview updated to 1 department

steps.action: Reload app and reopen Budget panel
steps.expected: Saved budget values persist after reload
steps.observed: Engineering spend cap/headcount cap persisted after reload

steps.expected: Org-level summary aggregates all department envelopes
steps.observed: Org-wide total budget became $2,000,000 (1,000,000 + 600,000 + 400,000) with headcount 0/10

evidence.network: POST /api/scenarios/:scenarioId/budgets (multiple) -> 201; aggregated totals rendered in Budget panel

assertions.title: Manager submits hire request with budget impact preview

steps.action: Fill in-budget Engineering hire request and inspect Budget Impact section
steps.expected: Preview shows current spend, projected spend, and utilization %
steps.observed: Budget Impact showed only 'Projected total cost: $100,000' plus chain routing; required current/projection/utilization breakdown was missing
Submission succeeded and request appeared in Pending queue

assertions.issues: Budget impact preview is incomplete vs contract requirements (missing current spend / projected spend / utilization%).

assertions.title: Over-budget and over-headcount hire requests are flagged

steps.action: Create HR envelope with budget $50,000 and headcount cap 0, then draft HR request for $100,000
steps.expected: Over-budget and over-headcount warnings are shown
steps.observed: No over-budget or over-headcount warning text appeared in request form

steps.expected: Request can be submitted but flagged
steps.observed: Request submitted, but queue showed standard pending entry with no over-limit flag indicator

assertions.issues: Expected over-budget/over-headcount warnings and flagging were not present.

assertions.title: Employee CRUD updates budget in real time

steps.expected: BudgetPanel headcount and comp increase without refresh
steps.observed: Actual Spend/Actual comp increased to $200,000 and headcount to 1
Actual Spend/Actual comp updated to $230,000 immediately

steps.expected: BudgetPanel headcount and comp decrease without refresh
steps.observed: BudgetPanel returned to headcount 0 and Actual Spend $0

evidence.network: POST /api/scenarios/:scenarioId/employees -> 201; PATCH /api/employees/:id -> 200; DELETE /api/employees/:id -> 200; GET /api/scenarios/:scenarioId/budgets refreshed in-session

steps.action: Create employee with salary $150,000 and equity $50,000
steps.observed: Budget panel showed Actual Spend/Actual comp = $200,000 and employee card displayed salary + equity

assertions.title: Employee edit propagates to all dependent surfaces

steps.action: Seed one employee in Engineering (isolated scenario), open DetailPanel, change department to Product, save
steps.expected: OrgChart node updates and dependent surfaces refresh
steps.observed: OrgChart node department changed Engineering -> Product immediately after save

steps.action: Inspect summary + budget surfaces in same session
steps.expected: HeadcountSummary remains consistent and BudgetPanel department breakdown shifts from Engineering to Product
steps.observed: HeadcountSummary stayed consistent at Total=1; BudgetPanel shifted spend/headcount from Engineering to Product (Engineering $0/0, Product $140,000/1)

steps.expected: Card appears in updated department column
steps.observed: Kanban grouped by Department showed employee card under Product

evidence.network: PATCH /api/employees/:id -> 200; GET /api/scenarios/:scenarioId/budgets -> 200; Kanban/OrgChart reflected updated department without page reload

frictions.description: Organization/scenario creation uses native prompt dialogs and was non-deterministic unless dialog accept was queued before clicking creation buttons.
frictions.resolution: Used `agent-browser dialog accept <value>` immediately before clicking New Organization/New Scenario.

frictions.description: Approval chain 'Save Chain' stayed disabled until the approver chip was explicitly toggled in the chain step.
frictions.resolution: Configured chain, filled required fields, then clicked the approver chip (current user) to enable Save Chain.

frictions.description: When approval chains are enabled, Add Employee is disabled in UI by design, requiring request-based flow for normal writes.
frictions.resolution: Used authenticated in-session API bootstrap to create isolated test employee for VAL-CROSS-017 setup, then validated behavior via UI.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 5 passed, 2 failed. Failing assertions: VAL-BUDGET-003 (incomplete budget impact preview fields) and VAL-BUDGET-004 (missing over-budget/headcount warnings and flagging behavior).
